notes: Open reading frame of at least 100 amino acids (300bp) suggests functional protein-coding region. Shorter ORFs may be tags, linkers, or peptides.
Canonical start codon required for translation initiation. Position must be in-frame with detected ORF.
In-frame stop codon terminates translation. TAA preferred in mammalian (ochre), TAG in bacteria (amber).

column9: Strong Kozak (GCC[AG]CCATGG) at ATG position is near-diagnostic of mammalian translation initiation site.
Adequate Kozak ([AG]CCATGG) supports mammalian translation but may have reduced efficiency.
Weak Kozak context may indicate suboptimal translation initiation or alternative start site.

notes: Strong RBS (AGGAGG) 4-12bp upstream of ATG is diagnostic of bacterial translation initiation.
Partial RBS consensus (GGAGG/AGGAG) supports bacterial translation but may have reduced efficiency.
Minimal RBS core (AGG) may indicate weak translation initiation site.
Complete expression unit with promoter driving ORF is near-diagnostic of functional CDS module.
Pol II promoter (CMV, EF1a, CAG, PGK, UbC) upstream defines 5' boundary of mammalian expression cassette.
Pol III promoters (U6, H1, 7SK) should not drive protein-coding genes. Negative weight indicates design error.
T7, lac, tac, araBAD promoters upstream define 5' boundary of bacterial expression cassette.
PolyA signal (bGH, SV40, hGH) downstream defines 3' boundary of mammalian CDS module.
Transcription terminator (T7 term, rrnB) downstream defines 3' boundary of bacterial CDS module.
Enhancer elements (CMV enhancer) upstream boost expression but not required for module boundary.
Introns between exons indicate mammalian genomic context or expression enhancement (intron-mediated enhancement).
5' UTR between promoter and ATG affects translation efficiency and mRNA stability.
3' UTR between stop codon and polyA affects mRNA stability and localization.
WPRE element boosts mRNA levels in lentiviral/AAV contexts. Not required for module detection.
Signal peptide (Igkappa, tPA, BiP) at N-terminus indicates secreted protein module.
Nuclear localization signal indicates nuclear-targeted protein.
Epitope tags (FLAG, HA, Myc, V5) indicate tagged protein for detection/purification.
Affinity tags (His6, Strep, MBP, GST) indicate protein for purification.
2A peptide (T2A, P2A, E2A, F2A) indicates polycistronic CDS with ribosomal skipping.
Internal ribosome entry site indicates bicistronic mRNA with cap-independent translation.
Flexible linker (GGGS, GGGGS repeats) between domains indicates fusion protein.
In-frame stop codon within ORF truncates protein. Strong negative indicator unless readthrough intended.
Frameshift mutation disrupts protein sequence. Strong negative indicator.
Missing ATG at annotated CDS start indicates annotation error or upstream translation initiation.
Promoter and CDS must be on same strand for functional expression.
Promoter and CDS on opposite strands indicates design error.
Canonical mammalian CDS module element order. Full order match is near-diagnostic.

features: promoter -> RBS -> ATG -> CDS -> stop -> terminator
notes: Canonical bacterial CDS module element order. Full order match is near-diagnostic.
pLannotate CDS annotation provides strong base evidence for protein-coding region.
Gene feature annotation supports presence of functional genetic element.
LTR boundaries indicate lentiviral context. CDS modules within LTRs are payload.
ITR boundaries indicate AAV context. CDS modules within ITRs are payload.
Complete mammalian CDS module signature with translation initiation context.
Complete bacterial CDS module signature with translation initiation context.
Selection marker (PuroR, NeoR, HygR, BSD) within CDS indicates selection cassette.
Reporter gene (GFP, mCherry, luciferase) within CDS indicates reporter cassette.
Nuclease (Cas9, Cas12, Cpf1) within CDS indicates editing cassette.
Recombinase (Cre, FLP, PhiC31) within CDS indicates recombination cassette.
Transactivator (tTA, rtTA, Gal4) within CDS indicates regulatory cassette.
Module boundaries should align to codon positions (divisible by 3 relative to ATG).
Multiple ORFs in same frame may indicate 2A-linked or read-through proteins.
Overlapping ORFs in different frames suggests annotation confusion or complex expression.
Methionine (M) at protein start confirms ATG translation initiation.
Stop codon (*) at expected protein end confirms ORF termination.
